title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty array of integers where every element appears twice except for one, find that single one. You must implement a solution with linear runtime complexity and use only constant extra space.
hints: XOR of a number with itself is 0.
XOR all numbers together; duplicates cancel out, leaving the unique one.

title: Reverse Bits
description: Reverse bits of a given 32 bits unsigned integer.
hints: Process the input bit by bit from right to left and build the reversed number.
Shift the result left and OR with the last bit of n, then shift n right.

title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Write a function that takes the binary representation of a 32-bit unsigned integer and returns the number of '1' bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
hints: Clear the lowest set bit in each iteration using n & (n - 1).
Count how many times you can do this before n becomes 0.
timeComplexity: O(k) where k is the number of set bits

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer n, return true if it is a power of two. An integer n is a power of two if there exists an integer x such that n == 2^x.
hints: A power of two in binary has exactly one set bit: 1, 10, 100, 1000, etc.
n & (n - 1) removes the lowest set bit. If n is a power of two, the result is 0.

title: Missing Number
description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing from the array. You must implement a solution with O(n) runtime complexity and O(1) extra space.
hints: XOR all indices 0..n with all array elements. Duplicates cancel.

title: Counting Bits
description: Given an integer n, return an array ans of length n + 1 where ans[i] is the number of 1's in the binary representation of i.
hints: Use dynamic programming: ans[i] = ans[i & (i - 1)] + 1.
i & (i - 1) removes the lowest set bit of i.

title: Hamming Distance
description: The Hamming distance between two integers is the number of positions at which the corresponding bits are different. Given two integers x and y, return the Hamming distance between them.
hints: XOR x and y to get a number where set bits indicate differing positions.
Count the number of 1 bits in the XOR result.

solutions.python:
def hammingDistance(x, y):
    xor = x ^ y
    count = 0
    while xor:
        xor &= xor - 1
        count += 1
    return count

title: Single Number II
description: Given an integer array nums where every element appears three times except for one, which appears exactly once. Find the single element and return it. You must implement a solution with linear runtime complexity and use only constant extra space.
hints: Count the number of 1s at each bit position across all numbers.
For each bit position, if the count of 1s is not divisible by 3, that bit belongs to the single number.

title: Single Number III
description: Given an integer array nums, in which exactly two elements appear only once and all the other elements appear exactly twice. Find the two elements that appear only once. You must implement a solution with linear runtime complexity and use only constant extra space.
hints: XOR all numbers to get xor = a ^ b where a and b are the two unique numbers.
Find any set bit in xor (e.g., xor & -xor). This bit differs between a and b.
Use that bit to partition all numbers into two groups and XOR each group separately.

title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Given two integers a and b, return the sum of the two integers without using the operators + and -.
hints: XOR gives the sum without carry: a ^ b.
AND gives the carry bits: (a & b) << 1. Repeat until carry is 0.
